title: allowWrite:[~/lab-glob-*] の * はリテラル -> lab-glob-XYZ に不一致。挙動はベースライン a と同一

probes.prompt: Run exactly this one bash command using the Bash tool: echo data > inside.txt — just run it, no commentary, no alternatives. If it is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.

Run exactly this one bash command using the Bash tool: echo data > $HOME/lab-fs-write/probe.txt — just run it, no commentary, no alternatives. If it is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.

Run exactly this one bash command using the Bash tool: echo data > $HOME/lab-glob-XYZ/probe.txt — just run it, no commentary, no alternatives. If it is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.

notes: DECIDES refactor-plan.md 2.3 / multi-repo-workspace.md: is sandbox.filesystem.allowWrite a glob or a literal path? allowWrite:[~/lab-glob-*] with w-glob writing to ~/lab-glob-XYZ. Measured DENIED (allow x ng): the * is LITERAL, so allowWrite only opens a directory literally named 'lab-glob-*', and lab-glob-XYZ does not match. Consequence: e's whole column equals baseline a's column (glob allowWrite is a no-op). Scope sandbox writes with full literal paths, not globs.
hypothesis: sandbox.filesystem paths are literal (no glob expansion). allowWrite:[~/lab-glob-*] does NOT open ~/lab-glob-XYZ, so w-glob EPERMs (allow x ng) and the column matches baseline a.
